Items.Key: unsafe string copy in evaluate_expression()|buffer overflow|cwe-120: classic buffer overflow|high|0,98|enhanced_calc.cpp|830|879|evaluate_expression()|double evaluate_expression(const std::string& expression) { if (expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_)) { strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str()); } else { string_utils::safe_string_copy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str(), sizeof(input_buffer_)); } ... }|the function copies user input directly into a fixed-size buffer without proper bounds checking. this could lead to buffer overflow vulnerabilities if the input is larger than the buffer size.|the function is called when a user inputs an expression in the main calculator interface.|implement a secure string copy mechanism that ensures the destination buffer is large enough to hold the entire string. use strncpy or similar safe functions.

unsafe string copy in tokenize_expression_for_parser()|buffer overflow|cwe-120: classic buffer overflow|high|0,98|enhanced_calc.cpp|755|798|tokenize_expression_for_parser()|std::vector<math_engine::token> tokenize_expression_for_parser(const std::string& expression) const { std::vector<math_engine::token> tokens; size_t i = 0; while (i < expression.size()) { ... if (expression[i] == '(') { tokens.emplace_back(math_engine::tokentype::paren_open, 0.0, std::string(1, expression[i]), i); ++i; } ... }|the function copies user input directly into a fixed-size buffer without proper bounds checking. this could lead to buffer overflow vulnerabilities if the input is larger than the buffer size.|the function is called when tokenizing expressions for parsing and evaluation.|implement a secure string copy mechanism that ensures the destination buffer is large enough to hold the entire string. use strncpy or similar safe functions.
